purpose: Make the source evidence to extracted design rule to workflow decision to generated slide surface chain visible and testable before native PPT code generation.

viewer_contract.first_read_boundary: The public slide can show compact human-readable evidence labels, but raw trace ids stay in manifest/viewer QA.
viewer_contract.qa_probe: A reviewer should be able to explain what each slide learned from data without opening the generator code.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Launch references teach a single product promise before trace detail; pacing references keep the opener as an orientation beat.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Show one launch promise and one native system surface before introducing detailed proof.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Lock the selected design-to-production usecase first, then expose only the cover content memory and two native evidence slots.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw a large editable launch promise, one native product-system motif, and a compact evidence-chain rail.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: cover slide with source-evidence rail, rule chip, workflow decision strip, and generated launch field
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer see that the cover learned a launch-orientation rule from data before code rendered the hero field?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm can name the usecase but cannot prove which source records or workflow gate shaped the cover.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Launch and hierarchy sources teach that the problem slide must contrast one weak prompt path against one selected product route.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Use a deterministic type ladder so the business failure reads before the workflow machinery.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Bind setup content memory, then require a visible prompt-loss object plus selected-route object before the gate can pass.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw a two-lane evidence chain: collapsed prompt path on the left, selected data/workflow route on the right.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: setup slide with before path, selected route, source-rule card, and workflow-gate card
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer see why prompt-only is weaker and which data rule creates the selected-route layout?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm may show a route label but lacks the typed hierarchy rule and density gate proof.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Hierarchy and spacing tutorials teach that before/after comparison needs asymmetric weight instead of equal cards.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Make the selected evidence route visibly larger than the weak prompt-only thumbnail.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Bind the contrast memory and only allow the selected thumbnail to become the dominant generated surface.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw a small weak-before block, a larger selected-after block, and a vertical evidence-chain spine between them.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: contrast slide with asymmetric before/after proof and inspectable source-rule-workflow spine
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer see which tutorial-derived rule made the selected-after surface larger and more legible?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm can label before/after but cannot justify the asymmetric weighting through source rules and workflow gates.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Tutorial and interaction records teach that proof must expose the memory surface and asset rail as product behavior, not as hidden metadata.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Show source cards, rule extraction, and asset obligations as one inspectable proof surface.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Require both content memory and visual asset rail to be present before the proof slide can claim the system learned from data.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw a four-step chain surface with source evidence, extracted rule, workflow decision, and generated slide surface columns.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: proof slide with complete four-step evidence-chain table and native asset obligation rail
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer trace one proof object from source record through rule and workflow into the generated surface?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm can show a proof panel but cannot bind asset obligations or source-rule provenance.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Video/audio references teach that climax attention should converge on one result object while trace detail stays secondary.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Reserve the largest field for one generated proof object and place the evidence chain as a supporting audit rail.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Bind climax content memory and hero-result slot before rendering a large editable result object.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw one oversized native hero result with a compact chain rail proving why it owns the room.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: climax slide with hero editable result object and compact evidence-chain rail
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer see that video/audio learning changed the climax into one dominant object rather than another diagram?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm can draw a big object but cannot prove it came from a video/audio climax rule and workflow gate.

slide_evidence_chains.source_evidence_summary: Interaction and pacing records teach that the close must hand off an inspectable path, not a generic next-step list.
slide_evidence_chains.extracted_design_rule: Show the release gate and trace handoff as the final product behavior.
slide_evidence_chains.workflow_decision: Keep public release blocked until the reviewer can inspect source, rule, workflow, and generated-surface lineage.
slide_evidence_chains.native_ppt_surface_instruction: Draw a release gate panel connected to a four-step evidence-chain handoff path.
slide_evidence_chains.generated_slide_surface: close slide with blocked release gate and inspectable trace handoff path
slide_evidence_chains.viewer_inspection_prompt: Can the reviewer see what remains blocked and what evidence would be inspected before public release?
slide_evidence_chains.negative_control_failure_mode: The bad arm can list release gates but cannot show the source-rule-workflow-output handoff path.

negative_control_contract.expected_failure: It may look superficially similar, but it cannot prove source evidence, extracted rule, workflow decision, or generated-surface lineage.
